name: codex_live_software_build
description: Live Codex suite that asks a local model behind Open Gate to build small, useful software artifacts with real files and lightweight self-checks.

cases.prompt: Build a small no-dependency Python command-line tool for personal CSV expense analysis. Work only in this folder. Create expense_report.py, sample_expenses.csv, and README.md. The CLI should accept a CSV path, print total spend, totals by category, and totals by month, and support --category NAME to filter. Run at least one command against the sample CSV and fix any errors you find. Keep the final answer short and include the commands you ran.

Build a no-dependency Python CLI for incident log triage. Work only in this folder. Create log_triage.py, sample_app.log, and README.md. The tool should parse lines with ISO timestamps and levels like INFO, WARN, ERROR, summarize counts by level, show the top repeated error messages, support --since YYYY-MM-DD, and support --json. Run at least two sample commands and fix any errors you find. Keep the final answer short and include the commands you ran.

Build a polished single-file habit tracker web app. Work only in this folder. Everything must be in index.html with no frameworks or external assets. It should let a user add habits, mark today complete, show a simple 7-day streak grid, delete habits, persist to localStorage, and look good on desktop and mobile. Add a tiny inline smoke test script or simple self-check comment, then verify the file exists and contains the expected app sections. Keep the final answer short and include the verification commands you ran.
